name: Fresh Corn Ice Cream with Almond Lace Cookie Fan
recipeIngredient: 3 Cups Fresh Sweet Corn, scraped from cob
3/4 cup ground almonds (process in a food processor)
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: cobs reserved and broken into 3-inch lengths
1. Combine corn and 1 cup half-and-half in a blender or food processor and process in short bursts to finely chop the corn.
2. Add the corn mixture to a saucepan along with the remaining cream, corn cobs, and vanilla bean. Bring to a simmer over moderate heat.
3. While the corn mixture is heating, beat together the egg yolks, sugar, honey, zest, and chile in a bowl until well combined.
4. Remove and discard the cobs from the corn-cream mixture and whisk it into the egg yolk mixture in a slow, steady stream.
5. Remove the vanilla bean, split it, and scrape the seeds into the mixture (Rinse the vanilla bean and use to flavor the sugar if desired!).
6. Return the mixture to the pan and cook the custard over low heat, stirring constantly, until it just begins to thicken (approximately 180 degrees). Be careful not to boil or eggs will curdle.
7. Strain through a fine mesh sieve, chill, and then freeze in an ice cream freezer according to manufacturer's directions.
Yield: 1 quart
Serve in chilled bowls with mint sprigs and Almond Lace Cookies, if desired.
Almond Lace Cookies
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Lightly butter and flour at least 2 baking sheets. Place sheets in refrigerator for at least 10 minutes.
1. In a large saute pan, combine the remaining ingredients. Stir over low heat with a wooden spoon until the butter melts and the ingredients are well mixed.
2. Place heaping teaspoons of the batter, about 5 inches apart on the prepared chilled sheets (the batter will spread during baking). Bake for 8 to 10 minutes or until evenly browned. Turn sheets halfway through baking.
3. Remove from the oven. Using a wide spatula, remove the cookies from the baking sheet and immediately shape as desired, into tightly rolled cigarettes, or allow to cool and serve flat.
Yield: 16-20 cookies
Store in airtight containers for 2 days or in freezer for up to a month.
